name: Valid $\ne$ Necessary: Diagnosing Latent Inefficiency in Chain-of-Thought
summary: arXiv:2607.11266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet it often incurs substanti...
description: arXiv:2607.11266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet it often incurs substantial computational costs due to over-reasoning: the generation of redundant, verbose, or irrelevant steps. While existing reasoning step evaluators effectively detect logical fallacies and factual error...
author: Daeyeop Lee, Hwanjo Yu
